title: helianthemum canum subsp. cantabricum
intro: Helianthemum canum subsp. cantabricum, also known as the Cantabrian rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a low-growing, woody shrub native to the Cantabrian Mountains in northern Spain. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The herbal preparations of Helianthemum canum subsp. cantabricum include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions, respiratory problems, and digestive issues.
